quotes: Never set running text below 12px.

relations.note: Defines the campaign-only exception to the general digital headline font treatment.
The line-height minimum applies alongside the email body type scale.

quotes: | Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only* | **Aptos** |
| Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos** |
| Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos |
| Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case; *Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only* | **Aptos** |
| Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos** |
| Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos |

rules.intent: Maintain a consistent type hierarchy while providing approved digital and Office fallbacks.
rules.rule_text: Apply the approved typography roles by surface: headline.font uses Agenda in print and Nunito Sans with bolder weights and Title Case in digital/email; body.font and fine_print.font use Nunito Sans, with Aptos as the Word/PPT/Excel fallback.

effect.subject: Agenda as live text in digital

quotes: Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only
Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only

rules.intent: Reserve the distinctive Agenda live-text treatment for the campaign headline.
rules.rule_text: Agenda may be used as live text in digital only for the campaign headline; other digital headlines use the approved Nunito Sans treatment.

quotes: **[GENERAL] Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`.

**[GENERAL] Email font stack (CSS):** `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`. Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

rules.intent: Provide consistent email typography with resilient rendering across clients.
rules.rule_text: Use the email font stack font.stack.email = 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif. Load Nunito Sans through @font-face or a Google Fonts link where supported so clients that strip webfonts fall back gracefully.

quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — **16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%) — must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — **16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%) — must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)
Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Keep email content readable and ensure visual styling communicates the intended reading order.
rules.rule_text: Use the approved email type hierarchy, including email.h1.type_scale = 28px/36px and email.body.type_scale = 16px/24px with a 16px minimum for body copy. Use secondary/supporting body sparingly and never for primary content. Footnote/reference text must be noticeably smaller than primary data and clearly separated. Never set running text below 12px; locked ISI sizing is excluded. Preserve a clear first-next-last hierarchy, and never use adjacent blocks with the same size and weight to represent different hierarchy levels.

quotes: **Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).
**Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Protect body-copy readability, especially when contrast conditions make text harder to scan.
rules.rule_text: Body copy must use body.line_height = at least 150%. For light text on dark backgrounds, increase it further to 160%, such as 16px/26px.
